subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #28 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Spring is Here
With the celebrations of Purim, Holi, Eid, and Easter, we are embracing our differences while also celebrating spring, hope, and renewal.  Spring brings growth, change, and new beginnings.
In the classroom, we are seeing children stretch themselves in new directions—trying new activities, forming deeper friendships, using more expressive language, and learning how to navigate challenges with increasing confidence. Growth at this age is not always linear; it often comes with moments of frustration, bursts of independence, and a strong desire to “do it myself.” These moments are an 
important and natural part of development, and we work alongside the children to support them with patience, encouragement, and clear expectations.
Ms. Linda returns soon and she always finds a way to connect the changes in the garden to your child's lives.
Mabel's Labels
Sale
Mabel's Labels is holding a sale in time for summer camps.  These labels are very useful for making sure items are returned to you.  Selecting SOCDS brings a portion of the sales to PEP.
PEP Yard Sale
April 19th
Reduce, Reuse, and Bloom: The SOCDS Earth Day Yard Sale
11:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Tentative)
Entry: $15 Suggested Donation to PEP (Additional donations gratefully accepted)
In honor of Earth Day, the Parent Enrichment Program (PEP) is thrilled to announce a special community event focused on sustainability and growth. We are hosting a Private Yard Sale to help our community reduce waste, reuse quality items, and raise vital funds for one of the most cherished parts of our school: Ms. Linda’s Gardening Program.
Our Goal: The 2026 Garden Grant
This year, PEP aims to gift $2,200 to Ms. Linda to fund her 2026 Garden Proposal. Ms. Linda envisions a new multi-sensory learning experience where our children can explore the interconnectivity of nature.
By navigating winding stone paths, trellises, and even a "hummingbird garden," the children will engage their senses and imagination in new ways. As Ms. Linda beautifully puts it, this program allows our children to "freely explore and learn from the natural environment," fostering a deep sensitivity to the world around them.
Pajama Day
Monday March 23rd
Monday is Pajama Day!!  This is always a favorite day.  This is in honor of Dr. Seuss'
The Sleep Book.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Mabel's Labels
Art with Ms. Jenny
Thank you to Ms. Jenny for supporting PreK Orange over the past couple of months, but everyone is happy to be back to art.
This week Ms. Jenny introduced the children to the art of Vincent Van Gogh and they explored using baby lines, spirals and bold colors with strong brush strokes.

@SOCDSMOMENTS
Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear to be comfortable on the playground for 30 minutes at a time.  Layers are great for the coming spring, as well as solid shoes.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your 
child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let 
me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
